finding.summary: Left channel is 3.6 dB louder than right channel, creating an off-center stereo image.
finding.probable_cause: Uneven gain between left and right input channels during recording.
finding.confidence_reason: Direct measurement of stereo balance shows a 3.6 dB left-right imbalance. The high correlation (0.82) suggests the source is essentially mono, so the imbalance is a gain mismatch rather than a deliberate stereo effect.

evidence_refs.description: Measured left-right level difference: 3.6 dB (left louder)
High correlation (0.82) confirms the channels are very similar, supporting a gain mismatch diagnosis

proposal.reason: Correct the left-right level imbalance by panning the stereo track slightly to the right, which attenuates the left channel relative to the right in REAPER's balance mode.
